steps.title: Use relative years in the model
steps.content: Let $t$ be years after $1996$. Then $1996$ is $t=0$, $2016$ is $t=20$, and $2020$ is $t=24$. The two known model values are $(0,61)$ and $(20,225)$, in thousands.

steps.title: Evaluate the line at 2020
steps.content: The table calculation gives $m=8.2$ and $b=61$, so $y=8.2t+61$. For $2020$, use $t=24$: $$y=8.2(24)+61=257.8.$$ To the nearest whole number, this is $258$ thousand.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The estimated population in $2020$ is $257.8$ thousand, which rounds to 258.
